title: jacquemontia rufa
intro: Jacquemontia rufa, also known as red manjack or red glory bower, is a type of flowering vine native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections. The leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
